response:
I'll estimate cell type proportions for each spot using unsupervised deconvolution methods. Since we don't have a reference dataset, I'll use Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) to identify latent cell type signatures and estimate their proportions in each spot.

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.decomposition import NMF
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import seaborn as sns

# Get slice 0 data
slice_0 = session.get_slice(0)
adata = slice_0.adata

print(f"Analyzing spot-level data: {adata.n_obs} spots × {adata.n_vars} genes")

# Prepare data for NMF
# Use log-normalized counts and select highly variable genes for better results
X = adata.X.toarray() if hasattr(adata.X, 'toarray') else adata.X

# Filter out genes with very low expression
gene_means = np.mean(X, axis=0)
high_expr_genes = gene_means > np.percentile(gene_means, 75)  # Top 25% expressed genes
X_filtered = X[:, high_expr_genes]
gene_names_filtered = adata.var_names[high_expr_genes]

print(f"Using {X_filtered.shape[1]} highly expressed genes for deconvolution")

# Apply NMF with different numbers of components (cell types)
n_components_list = [5, 7, 10, 12]
results = {}

for n_comp in n_components_list:
    print(f"\nTesting {n_comp} components...")
    
    # Fit NMF (removed alpha parameter)
    nmf = NMF(n_components=n_comp, random_state=42, max_iter=1000)
    W = nmf.fit_transform(X_filtered)  # Spot × CellType proportions
    H = nmf.components_  # CellType × Gene signatures
    
    # Calculate reconstruction error
    reconstruction_error = nmf.reconstruction_err_
    
    results[n_comp] = {
        'proportions': W,
        'signatures': H,
        'error': reconstruction_error,
        'model': nmf
    }
    
    print(f"Reconstruction error: {reconstruction_error:.4f}")

# Select best number of components based on reconstruction error
best_n_comp = min(results.keys(), key=lambda k: results[k]['error'])
print(f"\nBest number of components: {best_n_comp}")

# Get best results
best_proportions = results[best_n_comp]['proportions']
best_signatures = results[best_n_comp]['signatures']

# Normalize proportions to sum to 1 for each spot
proportions_normalized = best_proportions / best_proportions.sum(axis=1, keepdims=True)

# Create cell type names
celltype_names = [f'CellType_{i+1}' for i in range(best_n_comp)]

# Create proportions dataframe
proportions_df = pd.DataFrame(proportions_normalized, 
                            index=adata.obs_names,
                            columns=celltype_names)

print(f"\nEstimated cell type proportions (first 5 spots):")
print(proportions_df.head())

print(f"\nMean proportions across all spots:")
print(proportions_df.mean().round(3))

# Store results in adata
adata.obsm['deconv_weights'] = proportions_df
adata.uns['deconv_signatures'] = pd.DataFrame(best_signatures.T, 
                                            index=gene_names_filtered,
                                            columns=celltype_names)

# Assign dominant cell type to each spot
dominant_celltype = proportions_df.idxmax(axis=1)
adata.obs['celltype'] = dominant_celltype

print(f"\nDominant cell type distribution:")
print(dominant_celltype.value_counts())
```

```
Analyzing spot-level data: 3611 spots × 18002 genes
Using 4498 highly expressed genes for deconvolution

Testing 5 components...
Reconstruction error: 3913.4133

Testing 7 components...
Reconstruction error: 3798.6235

Testing 10 components...
Reconstruction error: 3671.5659

Testing 12 components...
Reconstruction error: 3611.5911

Best number of components: 12

Estimated cell type proportions (first 5 spots):
                    CellType_1  CellType_2  ...  CellType_11  CellType_12
AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1    0.412864    0.008531  ...     0.021220     0.166486
AAACAATCTACTAGCA-1    0.236210    0.041762  ...     0.022842     0.143264
AAACACCAATAACTGC-1    0.234225    0.215775  ...     0.427386     0.018120
AAACAGAGCGACTCCT-1    0.637822    0.012386  ...     0.009835     0.119220
AAACAGCTTTCAGAAG-1    0.512842    0.030649  ...     0.042879     0.033936

[5 rows x 12 columns]

Mean proportions across all spots:

CellType_2     0.045
CellType_3     0.056
CellType_4     0.06
... (output truncated)
```
```python
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import numpy as np

# Visualize the results

# 1. Plot reconstruction error vs number of components
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 12))

plt.subplot(2, 3, 1)
errors = [results[k]['error'] for k in n_components_list]
plt.plot(n_components_list, errors, 'bo-')
plt.xlabel('Number of Components')
plt.ylabel('Reconstruction Error')
plt.title('Model Selection: Reconstruction Error')
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# 2. Heatmap of mean proportions
plt.subplot(2, 3, 2)
mean_props = proportions_df.mean().values.reshape(1, -1)
sns.heatmap(mean_props, annot=True, fmt='.3f', 
           xticklabels=celltype_names, yticklabels=['Mean'],
           cmap='viridis', cbar_kws={'label': 'Proportion'})
plt.title('Mean Cell Type Proportions')

# 3. Distribution of dominant cell types
plt.subplot(2, 3, 3)
dominant_counts = dominant_celltype.value_counts()
plt.pie(dominant_counts.values, labels=dominant_counts.index, autopct='%1.1f%%')
plt.title('Dominant Cell Type Distribution')

# 4. Spatial plot of dominant cell types
plt.subplot(2, 3, 4)
# Create color map for cell types
colors = plt.cm.Set3(np.linspace(0, 1, len(celltype_names)))
celltype_colors = dict(zip(celltype_names, colors))

for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    mask = dominant_celltype == celltype
    if mask.sum() > 0:
        plt.scatter(adata.obs.loc[mask, 'x'], adata.obs.loc[mask, 'y'], 
                   c=[celltype_colors[celltype]], label=celltype, s=20, alpha=0.7)

plt.xlabel('X coordinate')
plt.ylabel('Y coordinate')
plt.title('Spatial Distribution of Dominant Cell Types')
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left')

# 5. Proportion diversity (entropy) across spots
plt.subplot(2, 3, 5)
# Calculate Shannon entropy for each spot
entropy = -np.sum(proportions_normalized * np.log(proportions_normalized + 1e-10), axis=1)
plt.scatter(adata.obs['x'], adata.obs['y'], c=entropy, s=20, cmap='plasma', alpha=0.7)
plt.colorbar(label='Shannon Entropy')
plt.xlabel('X coordinate')
plt.ylabel('Y coordinate')
plt.title('Cell Type Diversity per Spot')

# 6. Top marker genes for each cell type
plt.subplot(2, 3, 6)
# Get top 5 genes for each cell type
n_top_genes = 5
top_genes_per_type = []

for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    top_gene_indices = np.argsort(best_signatures[i])[-n_top_genes:]
    top_genes = gene_names_filtered[top_gene_indices]
    top_genes_per_type.extend([f"{celltype}_{gene}" for gene in top_genes])

# Create a simple text display
plt.text(0.1, 0.9, "Top Marker Genes:", fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', transform=plt.gca().transAxes)
y_pos = 0.8
for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    top_gene_indices = np.argsort(best_signatures[i])[-3:]  # Top 3 for display
    top_genes = gene_names_filtered[top_gene_indices]
    plt.text(0.1, y_pos, f"{celltype}: {', '.join(top_genes)}", 
            fontsize=10, transform=plt.gca().transAxes)
    y_pos -= 0.12

plt.axis('off')
plt.title('Top Marker Genes per Cell Type')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\nDeconvolution complete!")
print(f"Results stored in:")
print(f"- adata.obsm['deconv_weights']: Cell type proportions per spot")
print(f"- adata.obs['celltype']: Dominant cell type per spot")
print(f"- adata.uns['deconv_signatures']: Gene signatures per cell type")
```

```

Deconvolution complete!
Results stored in:
- adata.obsm['deconv_weights']: Cell type proportions per spot
- adata.obs['celltype']: Dominant cell type per spot
- adata.uns['deconv_signatures']: Gene signatures per cell type

```
*(1 plot(s) generated)*

**Analysis:**
The NMF deconvolution identified distinct cell type signatures and estimated their proportions in each spot. Key findings to examine: (1) The optimal number of cell types based on reconstruction error, (2) Spatial organization patterns of dominant cell types, (3) Areas with high cell type diversity (high entropy), and (4) The top marker genes that define each inferred cell type signature. This unsupervised approach provides a foundation for understanding tissue heterogeneity without requiring external reference data.

import pandas as pd

# Select best number of components based on reconstruction error

print(f"\nBest number of components: {best_n_comp}")

# Get best results

                                            columns=celltype_names)

# Assign dominant cell type to each spot

    top_genes_per_type.extend([f"{celltype}_{gene}" for gene in top_genes])

# Create a simple text display
plt.text(0.1, 0.9, "Top Marker Genes:", fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', transform=plt.gca().transAxes)
y_pos = 0.8
for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    top_gene_indices = np.argsort(best_signatures[i])[-3:]  # Top 3 for display
    top_genes = gene_names_filtered[top_gene_indices]
    plt.text(0.1, y_pos, f"{celltype}: {', '.join(top_genes)}", 
            fontsize=10, transform=plt.gca().transAxes)
    y_pos -= 0.12

plt.axis('off')
plt.title('Top Marker Genes per Cell Type')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\nDeconvolution complete!")
print(f"Results stored in:")
print(f"- adata.obsm['deconv_weights']: Cell type proportions per spot")
print(f"- adata.obs['celltype']: Dominant cell type per spot")
print(f"- adata.uns['deconv_signatures']: Gene signatures per cell type")

**Analysis:**
The NMF deconvolution identified distinct cell type signatures and estimated their proportions in each spot. Key findings to examine: (1) The optimal number of cell types based on reconstruction error, (2) Spatial organization patterns of dominant cell types, (3) Areas with high cell type diversity (high entropy), and (4) The top marker genes that define each inferred cell type signature. This unsupervised approach provides a foundation for understanding tissue heterogeneity without requiring external reference data.


I'll estimate cell type proportions for each spot using unsupervised deconvolution methods. Since we don't have a reference dataset, I'll use Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) to identify latent cell type signatures and estimate their proportions in each spot.

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.decomposition import NMF
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import seaborn as sns

# Get slice 0 data
slice_0 = session.get_slice(0)
adata = slice_0.adata

print(f"Analyzing spot-level data: {adata.n_obs} spots × {adata.n_vars} genes")

# Prepare data for NMF
# Use log-normalized counts and select highly variable genes for better results
X = adata.X.toarray() if hasattr(adata.X, 'toarray') else adata.X

# Filter out genes with very low expression
gene_means = np.mean(X, axis=0)
high_expr_genes = gene_means > np.percentile(gene_means, 75)  # Top 25% expressed genes
X_filtered = X[:, high_expr_genes]
gene_names_filtered = adata.var_names[high_expr_genes]

print(f"Using {X_filtered.shape[1]} highly expressed genes for deconvolution")

# Apply NMF with different numbers of components (cell types)
n_components_list = [5, 7, 10, 12]
results = {}

for n_comp in n_components_list:
    print(f"\nTesting {n_comp} components...")
    
    # Fit NMF (removed alpha parameter)
    nmf = NMF(n_components=n_comp, random_state=42, max_iter=1000)
    W = nmf.fit_transform(X_filtered)  # Spot × CellType proportions
    H = nmf.components_  # CellType × Gene signatures
    
    # Calculate reconstruction error
    reconstruction_error = nmf.reconstruction_err_
    
    results[n_comp] = {
        'proportions': W,
        'signatures': H,
        'error': reconstruction_error,
        'model': nmf
    }
    
    print(f"Reconstruction error: {reconstruction_error:.4f}")

# Select best number of components based on reconstruction error
best_n_comp = min(results.keys(), key=lambda k: results[k]['error'])
print(f"\nBest number of components: {best_n_comp}")

# Get best results
best_proportions = results[best_n_comp]['proportions']
best_signatures = results[best_n_comp]['signatures']

# Normalize proportions to sum to 1 for each spot
proportions_normalized = best_proportions / best_proportions.sum(axis=1, keepdims=True)

# Create cell type names
celltype_names = [f'CellType_{i+1}' for i in range(best_n_comp)]

# Create proportions dataframe
proportions_df = pd.DataFrame(proportions_normalized, 
                            index=adata.obs_names,
                            columns=celltype_names)

print(f"\nEstimated cell type proportions (first 5 spots):")
print(proportions_df.head())

print(f"\nMean proportions across all spots:")
print(proportions_df.mean().round(3))

# Store results in adata
adata.obsm['deconv_weights'] = proportions_df
adata.uns['deconv_signatures'] = pd.DataFrame(best_signatures.T, 
                                            index=gene_names_filtered,
                                            columns=celltype_names)

# Assign dominant cell type to each spot
dominant_celltype = proportions_df.idxmax(axis=1)
adata.obs['celltype'] = dominant_celltype

print(f"\nDominant cell type distribution:")
print(dominant_celltype.value_counts())
```

```python
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import numpy as np

# Visualize the results

# 1. Plot reconstruction error vs number of components
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 12))

plt.subplot(2, 3, 1)
errors = [results[k]['error'] for k in n_components_list]
plt.plot(n_components_list, errors, 'bo-')
plt.xlabel('Number of Components')
plt.ylabel('Reconstruction Error')
plt.title('Model Selection: Reconstruction Error')
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# 2. Heatmap of mean proportions
plt.subplot(2, 3, 2)
mean_props = proportions_df.mean().values.reshape(1, -1)
sns.heatmap(mean_props, annot=True, fmt='.3f', 
           xticklabels=celltype_names, yticklabels=['Mean'],
           cmap='viridis', cbar_kws={'label': 'Proportion'})
plt.title('Mean Cell Type Proportions')

# 3. Distribution of dominant cell types
plt.subplot(2, 3, 3)
dominant_counts = dominant_celltype.value_counts()
plt.pie(dominant_counts.values, labels=dominant_counts.index, autopct='%1.1f%%')
plt.title('Dominant Cell Type Distribution')

# 4. Spatial plot of dominant cell types
plt.subplot(2, 3, 4)
# Create color map for cell types
colors = plt.cm.Set3(np.linspace(0, 1, len(celltype_names)))
celltype_colors = dict(zip(celltype_names, colors))

for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    mask = dominant_celltype == celltype
    if mask.sum() > 0:
        plt.scatter(adata.obs.loc[mask, 'x'], adata.obs.loc[mask, 'y'], 
                   c=[celltype_colors[celltype]], label=celltype, s=20, alpha=0.7)

plt.xlabel('X coordinate')
plt.ylabel('Y coordinate')
plt.title('Spatial Distribution of Dominant Cell Types')
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left')

# 5. Proportion diversity (entropy) across spots
plt.subplot(2, 3, 5)
# Calculate Shannon entropy for each spot
entropy = -np.sum(proportions_normalized * np.log(proportions_normalized + 1e-10), axis=1)
plt.scatter(adata.obs['x'], adata.obs['y'], c=entropy, s=20, cmap='plasma', alpha=0.7)
plt.colorbar(label='Shannon Entropy')
plt.xlabel('X coordinate')
plt.ylabel('Y coordinate')
plt.title('Cell Type Diversity per Spot')

# 6. Top marker genes for each cell type
plt.subplot(2, 3, 6)
# Get top 5 genes for each cell type
n_top_genes = 5
top_genes_per_type = []

for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    top_gene_indices = np.argsort(best_signatures[i])[-n_top_genes:]
    top_genes = gene_names_filtered[top_gene_indices]
    top_genes_per_type.extend([f"{celltype}_{gene}" for gene in top_genes])

# Create a simple text display
plt.text(0.1, 0.9, "Top Marker Genes:", fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', transform=plt.gca().transAxes)
y_pos = 0.8
for i, celltype in enumerate(celltype_names):
    top_gene_indices = np.argsort(best_signatures[i])[-3:]  # Top 3 for display
    top_genes = gene_names_filtered[top_gene_indices]
    plt.text(0.1, y_pos, f"{celltype}: {', '.join(top_genes)}", 
            fontsize=10, transform=plt.gca().transAxes)
    y_pos -= 0.12

plt.axis('off')
plt.title('Top Marker Genes per Cell Type')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\nDeconvolution complete!")
print(f"Results stored in:")
print(f"- adata.obsm['deconv_weights']: Cell type proportions per spot")
print(f"- adata.obs['celltype']: Dominant cell type per spot")
print(f"- adata.uns['deconv_signatures']: Gene signatures per cell type")
```
